behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is handler auth + forwarding handoff (MOCK-MISS), not only pure HMAC math. An integration test wiring HTTP handler, signature verifier, and queue adapter catches accidental enqueue on auth failure. Rejected higher levels: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement in behavior text, and no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Start the handler with real verifier and a test queue adapter in-process; send a request with valid body but mismatched `X-Signature-256`; assert HTTP 401 and assert queue write was never called.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is an ordering guarantee across modules (HTTP handler -> persistent queue -> response), so Unit is too low (MOCK-MISS). Integration with a real local persistence-backed queue (e.g., sqlite/file) can catch wrong await/order logic. Rejected System: behavior does not require real network or OS-only primitive beyond local module wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Use an in-process app and persistence-backed test queue; instrument timestamps/hooks so queue `enqueue` commit is observable; send webhook and assert queue commit happens before response `200` is emitted.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is about decoupling handler response path from downstream async worker completion, which is module interaction (MOCK-MISS). A deterministic in-process integration test is sufficient. Rejected System: text does not require real network/subprocess semantics; rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + downstream worker where worker intentionally blocks (e.g., unresolved promise for N seconds); send request and assert response returns immediately while worker is still running.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly depends on wall-clock latency under concurrency and backlog pressure (REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE). In-process fakes can miss event-loop contention, socket scheduling, and real request concurrency effects. No AI/ML dependency, so not Agentic/Workflow.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real Node server process; generate concurrent requests while earlier events trigger slow downstream work; measure end-to-end HTTP latency per request and assert responses stay under 3 seconds.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness is at handler/idempotency store/queue boundary (MOCK-MISS). Integration catches real duplicate-handoff bugs (e.g., missing uniqueness check in wiring). Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not require real network/subprocess, and no AI/ML output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: With handler, idempotency mechanism, and persistent queue wired in-process, send the same event ID twice; assert first request enqueues once and second does not create an additional queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is translation of queue backpressure signal into HTTP contract across module boundary. Integration is enough: queue adapter can deterministically return `full` and handler mapping can be verified. Rejected System: no requirement for real network failure mode; rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with queue component configured to report capacity-full on enqueue; send webhook and assert HTTP 503 plus `Retry-After` header value is present and correctly formatted.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This targets queue + persistence interaction, not just a pure function; Unit risks missing storage-layer enforcement bugs (MOCK-MISS). Integration with real local persistence validates true capacity behavior. Rejected System: behavior text does not require external network/subprocess/OS-specific primitive beyond local persistence.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real persistent queue against test local storage with capacity N; enqueue N events successfully, then enqueue N+1 and assert explicit rejection/error and no persisted overflow record.
